most_recent_game.recap_text:
MEMPHIS, Tenn. –
The University of Memphis (5-14) baseball team dropped the series finale to Eastern Illinois (8-8) by a score of 25-14 on Sunday afternoon at FedExPark Avron Fogelman Field.
The two teams combined for 39 runs and 40 hits in the offensive battle, with Memphis collecting 21 hits but unable to keep pace with Eastern Illinois' 25-run output. The Panthers plated runs in seven different innings and used a nine-run fourth inning to break the game open.
James Smith IV
led the Tigers offensively, going 3-for-6 with a home run and six RBIs, while
Michael Gupton
,
Javon Hernandez
and
Trae Cassidy
each recorded at least three hits.
Jack Little
also homered for Memphis as the Tigers produced runs in four different innings.
Eastern Illinois reliever Jarrett Kinder earned the win after throwing four scoreless innings to close the game. Memphis starter
Seth Garner
suffered the loss after allowing five runs in 2.1 innings of work.
Memphis will open conference play next weekend when they travel to Tulane. First pitch on Friday is scheduled for 6:35 p.m. in New Orleans, Louisiana.
HOW IT HAPPENED
Eastern Illinois opened the scoring in the second inning when Mike O'Conor launched a two-run home run to left field. RBI hits from Chris Worcester and Kyree Alexander extended the Panthers' lead to 4-0.
The Panthers added another run in the third on a bases-loaded walk before Memphis responded in the bottom half of the inning. Designated hitter
James Smith IV
blasted a three-run homer to left field, and first baseman
Tyler Harrington
later added an RBI single to cut the deficit to 5-4.
Eastern Illinois erupted for nine runs in the fourth inning, taking advantage of walks, wild pitches and multiple hits to stretch the lead to 14-4.
Memphis answered with four runs of its own in the bottom of the inning. Smith IV doubled home two runs and center fielder
Michael Gupton
added an RBI double to trim the margin to 14-8.
Left fielder
Jack Little
led off the fifth inning with a solo home run, and Smith IV added an RBI single later in the frame to pull Memphis within 15-10.
The Tigers continued to rally in the sixth. Right fielder
Cade Hitson
delivered a two-run triple and catcher
Trae Cassidy
followed with an RBI double to cut the deficit to 16-14.
Eastern Illinois regained control late in the game, scoring six runs in the eighth inning highlighted by a three-run homer from Chris Worcester.
The Panthers added three more runs in the ninth inning, including a two-run double by Luke Melton, to secure the 25-14 victory.
NOTABLES
James Smith IV
went 3-for-6 with six RBIs, including a three-run homer and a double.
Smith finished the series 9-for-15 with 16 RBIs.
Hit his fifth homer of the season and third of the series, bringing his total to five on the year (2
nd
most on the team).
Now has 10 extra-base hits on the year.
Recorded his sixth multi-hit game this season.
Extended his seven-game hitting streak.
21 hits are the most in a game for the Tigers since logging 23 against Western Illinois on March 1, 2020.
All nine starters for the Tigers recorded at least one hit for the first time since April 6, 2019, against Southern.
Center fielder
Michael Gupton
finished the day 4-for-4 at the plate with a double.
Gupton is the first player since
Shane Cox
at Arkansas State on March 11, 2025, to record four hits in a game for Memphis.
His four hits bring his season total to 21 and mark a Memphis career high.
Jack Little
got his first start at left field and finished the day 2-for-5 and his second home run of the season.
Right fielder
Cade Hitson
hit a two-RBI triple on Sunday.
The triple is Memphis' third of the season.
Hitson has recorded a hit in all five games he has played in thus far.
Shortstop
Javon Hernandez
batted ninth and went 3-for-3 with a double, two walks and three runs scored.
The Tigers' usual leadoff hitter, Hernandez batted ninth for just the second time this season.
After a double and a single on Sunday, second baseman
Shane Cox
continued his streak of reaching base safely in every game thus far.
Cox has recorded at least one hit in 17 of 19 games this season.
Cox leads the Tigers in batting average (.382), hits (29), doubles (8), and extra-base hits (11).
Memphis shortstop
Michael Politte
entered the game as a pitcher in the ninth inning due to a depleted Tiger bullpen.
Politte finished the game for Memphis on the mound, recording two outs, allowing one run on two hits and struck out the first batter he faced.

UP NEXT
Memphis will travel to Tulane to open American Conference play next weekend. First pitch is set for 6:35 p.m. Friday (Mar. 20) and Saturday (Mar. 21), with Sunday's first pitch slated for 1:05 p.m.
HOW TO FOLLOW THE TIGERS:
For complete information on Memphis Tiger Baseball, visit
www.GoTigersGo.com
and follow the team's social media channels on
Twitter
,
Instagram
and
Facebook
